---
title: "get_candlesticks"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_candlesticks()`

Get Market Candlesticks

## Usage

```r
get_candlesticks(
  ticker,
  series_ticker,
  period_interval = 60,
  start = NULL,
  end = NULL,
  demo = FALSE
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `ticker` | Character. The market ticker. |
| `series_ticker` | Character. The series ticker for this market. |
| `period_interval` | Integer. Candle period in minutes: 1, 60, or 1440. |
| `start` | Date, character, or integer. Start date/timestamp. Accepts "2026-02-01", as.Date(), or Unix seconds. Defaults to 30 days ago. |
| `end` | Date, character, or integer. End date/timestamp. Accepts "2026-02-07", as.Date(), or Unix seconds. Defaults to now. |
| `demo` | Logical. Use demo environment if TRUE. |

## Value

A tibble with OHLC data: time, open, high, low, close, volume.

## Description

Retrieves historical OHLC price data for a specific market.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
# Using dates (simple)
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  start = "2026-02-06",
  end = "2026-02-08"
)

# Using period interval
candles <- get_candlesticks(
  ticker = "KXHIGHNY-26FEB07-B26.5",
  series_ticker = "KXHIGHNY",
  period_interval = 1  # 1-minute candles
)

## End(Not run)
```

## See also

Other markets: 
`get_market()`,
`get_markets()`,
`get_orderbook()`,
`get_trades()`
